spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the headline capability the spec promises: navigator.storage.estimate() returns identical quota in normal and incognito. Aligned with the chromestatus motivation.

interactive.rationale: Live interactive controls present and wired up — passes the no-static-card rule.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is well covered; edge cases / failure modes from the spec (see open questions) are not exercised on this concept page.

references.rationale: Has a see-also block but no explicit chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link on this concept page — the self-heal slug recovery relies on that link being present.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'browser version below fix' simulation
openQuestions.detail: Visitors on Chrome 151+ will always read 'indistinguishable'. Adding a mode that synthesises the pre-fix quota lets the lab demonstrate the attack succeeding so the value of the fix is visible without needing to find an old browser.

openQuestions.title: Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page
openQuestions.detail: The self-heal recovery script relies on every concept page carrying the canonical chromestatus link in a see-also or references block. Add it: https://chromestatus.com/feature/5143175657291776

summary: Classic Incognito vectors (quota threshold, RAM-fraction, webkitRequestFileSystem) probed live. Main gaps: Add a 'browser version below fix' simulation; Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page.
